chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ طَلَّقَ وَ فَرَّقَ بَيْنَ الشُّهُودِ أَوْ طَلَّقَ بِحَضْرَةِ قَوْمٍ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لَهُمُ اشْهَدُوا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – The one who divorces and separates between the witnesses, or divorces in the presence of a group and does not say to them, ‘Bear witness’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/122)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/122)
text: سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَهُرَتِ امْرَأَتُهُ مِنْ حَيْضِهَا فَقَالَ فُلَانَةُ طَالِقٌ وَ قَوْمٌ يَسْمَعُونَ كَلَامَهُ وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لَهُمُ اشْهَدُوا أَ يَقَعُ الطَّلَاقُ عَلَيْهَا قَالَ نَعَمْ هَذِهِ شَهَادَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Safwan Bin Yahya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘He<sup>asws</sup> was asked about a man whose wife was pure from her menstruation, so he said, ‘So and so woman is divorced’, and a group was listening to his speech, and he did not say to them, ‘Bear witness’. Does the divorce occur upon her?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, this is witnessing’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Safwan ibn Yahya who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about the case of a man whose wife becomes clean of Hayd (menses) and he says this: ‘So and so is ‘Taliq’ (let go, divorced) and a people hears his statement but he does not ask them to bear witness: if divorce takes place or not, he (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, is bearing witness.’”
